title: Design System: Double Play at Wrigley

display.purpose: Hero section headlines and large section callouts only. DM Serif Display — never at body or UI sizes.

headline.purpose: Section headings, unit names, page-level titles. Manrope 700 with tight letter-spacing.

title.purpose: Card titles, event headings, booking panel headers.

body.purpose: All running prose. 65-75ch max-width. Line-height 1.7 for long passages.

label.purpose: Eyebrow labels above section headings, form field labels, trust badges, meta info. Always uppercase.

shadows.name: shadow-card
shadows.purpose: Unit cards, event cards, neighborhood tiles — the standard container lift.

shadows.name: shadow-panel
shadows.purpose: Hero booking panel and focal-point callouts. Heavier directional lift for primary conversion surfaces.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: State transitions: button hover, link color changes.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Two-column layout collapses for neighborhood/overview grids

components.name: Primary CTA Button
components.description: The main booking and search action. Orange background, white text. Used for 'Search & Book', 'Check Availability', 'Read the full guide'.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-block; background: #E85A2C; color: #ffffff; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 16px; letter-spacing: 0.005em; border: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s; text-decoration: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #d04a1f; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E85A2C; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Navy Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action button used on unit cards ('View Apartment'). Navy background, white text.
components.css: .ds-btn-navy { display: block; text-align: center; background: #15375c; color: #ffffff; padding: 17px 16px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 15px; letter-spacing: 0.005em; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.18s; } .ds-btn-navy:hover { background: #0f2a48; } .ds-btn-navy:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #15375c; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Outline Accent Button
components.description: Secondary CTA that lives near orange primaries. White background, orange border and text. Used on event cards ('Book for this date').
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: block; text-align: center; background: #ffffff; color: #E85A2C; border: 1.5px solid #E85A2C; padding: 12px 14px; border-radius: 6px; font-family: 'Manrope', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 14px; letter-spacing: 0.005em; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.18s, color 0.18s; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #E85A2C; color: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #E85A2C; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Unit Card
components.description: The main apartment listing card. White background, 8px radius, shadow-card. Image at top (16/10 ratio), content below with heading, tagline, and bottom-pinned CTA.
components.html:
The Catbird Seat

Top-floor views, two bedrooms, steps from the marquee.

View Apartment
components.css: .ds-unit-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(15,42,72,0.04), 0 12px 28px -12px rgba(15,42,72,0.18); overflow: hidden; display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-unit-card__image { aspect-ratio: 16/10; background: #e8eaee; } .ds-unit-card__body { padding: 28px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; flex: 1; } .ds-unit-card__title { font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 24px; letter-spacing: -0.015em; color: #15375c; margin: 0 0 12px; } .ds-unit-card__tagline { color: #6b7585; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0 0 auto; padding-bottom: 28px; }

components.name: Event Card
components.description: Event listing card with a date-tile at top. White background, 14px radius. DM Serif Display for the day numeral — the one exception to the brand-only serif rule.
components.html:
SAT
14
JUN

Cubs vs. Cardinals

Book for this date
components.css: .ds-event-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 14px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(15,42,72,0.04), 0 12px 28px -12px rgba(15,42,72,0.18); border: 1px solid #eef0f3; padding: 26px 24px 24px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-event-card__date { background: #f1f4f9; border-radius: 10px; padding: 14px 10px; text-align: center; margin-bottom: 18px; color: #15375c; } .ds-event-card__dow { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.2em; color: #E85A2C; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-event-card__day { font-family: 'DM Serif Display', Georgia, serif; font-size: 46px; line-height: 1; margin: 2px 0; letter-spacing: -0.02em; } .ds-event-card__mon { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.2em; color: #15375c; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-event-card__title { font-weight: 700; color: #15375c; font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: -0.01em; margin: 0 0 14px; flex: 1; }

components.name: Trust Badge Pill
components.description: Horizontal pill for trust signals (Superhost, Premier Host, 5.0 Rating). White background, line border, 999px radius.
components.css: .ds-trust-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 12px 20px; background: #ffffff; border: 1.5px solid #e6e8ec; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; color: #15375c; letter-spacing: 0.02em; } .ds-trust-badge__dot--orange { color: #E85A2C; font-size: 16px; } .ds-trust-badge__dot--green { color: #23a06b; font-size: 14px; }

components.name: Form Input
components.description: Booking form text/date/select input. White background, line border, 6px radius, 48px height.
components.css: .ds-field { margin-bottom: 18px; } .ds-field__label { display: block; font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.16em; color: #15375c; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-field__wrap { display: flex; align-items: center; border: 1.5px solid #e6e8ec; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0 14px; height: 48px; background: #ffffff; transition: border-color 0.15s; } .ds-field__wrap:focus-within { border-color: #15375c; } .ds-field__input { width: 100%; border: 0; outline: 0; background: transparent; font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; color: #1c2433; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; }

components.name: Section Eyebrow Label
components.description: Orange uppercase label that precedes a section headline. The system's most recognizable punctuation — always appears before h2 headings that open a new chapter.
components.html:
Why Book Direct

Skip the middleman. Stay direct.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.22em; color: #E85A2C; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 14px; } .ds-section-headline { font-family: 'DM Serif Display', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 400; font-size: 40px; letter-spacing: -0.015em; color: #15375c; margin: 0 0 16px; line-height: 1.15; }

narrative.overview:
Worn, specific, trustworthy. The design feels like something a neighborhood regular hands you with a recommendation scrawled on the back. Not polished to a corporation's shine. Not rustic for rustic's sake. Just confident, genuine, and earned.

The palette pairs deep Wrigley Night Navy against a live Game Day Orange. Two colors that carry the neighborhood. The serif display type (DM Serif Display) shows up at headline scale only. Manrope handles everything else. Same two typefaces, same shadow vocabulary, same orange-or-navy button decision, every time.

This system explicitly rejects: corporate hotel chain sterility, Airbnb/VRBO listing-card blankness, cheap vacation rental clutter, and the trendy startup aesthetic.
keyCharacteristics: Two typefaces, no exceptions: DM Serif Display (display only) and Manrope (everything else)
Navy as the dominant brand color; orange reserved for action and emphasis
Ambient navy-tinted shadows — every surface lifts from the page the same way
Eyebrow labels in orange uppercase: the system's most recognizable punctuation
Background cycling: white, bg-soft, bg-warm, and full navy — each section signals a new chapter

rules.name: The One Action Rule
rules.body: Orange is the site's action color. It appears on booking buttons, eyebrow labels, and call-to-action links. It does not appear on decorative elements.

rules.name: The Navy Background Rule
rules.body: When a section uses navy as its full-bleed background, body text becomes white and secondary text becomes rgba(255,255,255,0.9). Orange still means action.

rules.name: The Navy-Tinted Shadow Rule
rules.body: All box-shadows use rgba(15,42,72,...) as their color. rgba(0,0,0,...) shadows are forbidden.

rules.name: The Two-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Every shadow consists of exactly two layers: a tight ambient layer and a larger directional layer. Never one, never three.

rules.name: The Serif Guest Rule
rules.body: DM Serif Display appears at display scale and on isolated date numerals in the events component. Never at body or label sizes.

rules.name: The Tight Heading Rule
rules.body: All Manrope headings use negative letter-spacing (-0.01em to -0.015em). Headings that feel loose are placeholders.

dos: Use SHADOW_CARD on all card-level containers and SHADOW_PANEL on the booking panel — never swapped
Use Game Day Orange for all primary booking CTAs
Use DM Serif Display only at display scale
Use orange eyebrow labels to open sections
Apply negative letter-spacing to all Manrope headings
Write in first-person plural throughout UI copy
donts: Don't use glassmorphism or backdrop-filter on UI elements
Don't use rgba(0,0,0,...) or #000 in any shadow
Don't make orange decorative — orange means action
Don't use DM Serif Display at body, label, or UI sizes
Don't build side-stripe borders (colored border-left wider than 1px)
Don't create identical card grids with the same internal structure
Don't design like a corporate hotel chain
Don't design like a cheap vacation rental site
Don't use trendy startup aesthetics (gradient blobs, neon accents, glassmorphism)
